text:
May 26, 2025:
Equities pushed higher today led by Nasdaq (+1.19%), reflecting continued appetite for tech and growth names. The S&P 500 crossed 7,500, a significant psychological level worth monitoring for resistance. Dow lagged slightly (-0.23%), suggesting rotation away from value/cyclicals.
The rates picture is quite notable as the 10Y yield dropped 1.43% on the day to 4.49% while the 2Y fell only 0.08% to 3.58%, steepening the curve further to a +91bps spread. This is the market pricing in future Fed cuts while long-end inflation expectations remain sticky, evidenced by the 30Y holding above 5%. Worth watching whether the long end continues to sell off independently - a 30Y above 5% historically pressures equity multiples.
Gold at $4,500 remains elevated despite today's slight pullback, signaling persistent macro uncertainty and dollar weakness where EUR/USD at 1.1643 confirms USD softness broadly. Oil down 2.81% to $93.89 is worth monitoring; a continued decline could ease inflation pressure and give the Fed more room to cut.
VIX at 17 shows moderate and ticking up slightly despite the equity rally. The divergence between rising equities and rising VIX is a subtle warning sign; options markets may be hedging against near-term event risk. Key catalysts to watch this week include any Fed speakers, PCE inflation data, and oil inventory numbers.
